title: Proxmox VE / Ceph Pool NEARFULL and OSD Space Pressure
exclusions: Not VMware vSphere or vSAN (UC-19.2.27 / UC-19.2.17 / UC-19.2.23), not Nutanix (UC-19.2.25), not Azure Stack HCI or S2D, not Cisco HyperFlex, not Pure FlashArray. Not ZFS-only PVE with no Ceph, though ZFS is still valid for local-only VMs—this search expects Ceph pool and status lines. Not guest OS in-guest file free % without Ceph context. CIM and ES datamodels are not used. Not a replacement for Ceph MGR/telemetry or your vendor support bundle when opening a Ceph bug. Not application-level SLOs inside guests or backup retention math on external appliances unless you extend the data model. If you have no proxmox:ceph:osd, do not expect worst_osd_* to drive severity beyond pool-only math—ingest the OSD path or the nix fallbacks in prerequisites.
evidence: Saved report or alert `uc_19_2_33_pve_ceph_capacity` in the hci (or ceph) index with 13+ month retention, proxmox_ceph_owner in version control, sample curl outputs archived with change records, and alert run history in audit_evidence or the ITSM. Attach a Datacenter Ceph screen capture for a sampled row during quarterly control testing.
dataSources: PRIMARY: (index=hci OR index=ceph) and sourcetypes `proxmox:ceph:pool` (PVE `GET /api2/json/cluster/ceph/pools` — per-pool: pool_name, size, min_size, pg_num, crush_rule, bytes_used, max_avail, percent_used, target_size_ratio, plus your envelope cluster_name) OR `proxmox:ceph:osd` (per-node `GET /api2/json/nodes/{node}/ceph/osd` plus normalization: osd_id, up, in, weight, reweight, kb, kb_used, kb_avail, utilization, var, host) OR `proxmox:ceph:status` (`GET /api2/json/cluster/ceph/status` – health.status, health.checks for POOL_NEARFULL, POOL_BACKFILLFULL, POOL_FULL, OSD_NEARFULL, OSD_BACKFILLFULL, OSD_FULL, mon_quorum, pgmap bytes fields). HEC is custom; authenticate with PVE `Authorization: PVEAPIToken=user@realm!tokenid=secret`. OPTIONAL: `proxmox:cluster:status` from `GET /api2/json/cluster/status` for node liveness, joined in dashboards. TERTIARY: on-node `ceph df detail --format=json` and `ceph osd df --format=json` via Splunk_TA_nix as `proxmox:ceph:df` and `proxmox:ceph:osd_df` when the API path is thin.
app: Custom HEC app (for example `TA_pve_ceph` on a Heavy Forwarder) plus a Python 3.10+ poller using the PVE REST as above; use Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833) only if you add scripted `ceph` CLI inputs for tertiaries. Store PVE and HEC secrets in a vault, not in etc apps.
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.33 — Proxmox PVE Ceph: pool+OSD capacity vs mon_osd_nearfull_ratio mon_osd_backfillfull_ratio mon_osd_full_ratio; PVE API HEC (proxmox:ceph:pool, proxmox:ceph:osd, proxmox:ceph:status). NOT vSAN, NOT Nutanix, NOT S2D. Pollers emit snake_case; coalesce for camelCase. ETA uses 24h streamstats; freeze demotes only when not HEALTH_ERR AND below full ratios; AND OR boolean keywords in logic.")`
(index=hci OR index=ceph) sourcetype="proxmox:ceph:pool" earliest=-2h@h
| eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, pve_cluster, ""))
| eval pool_name=toString(coalesce(pool_name, poolName, name, ""))
| eval pool_t=tonumber(toString(coalesce(percent_used, percentUsed, "0")), 10)
| eval pool_percent_used=if((isnotnull(pool_t)) AND (pool_t>1), round(pool_t/100, 6), round(coalesce(pool_t, 0), 6))
| sort 0 + cluster_name, pool_name, _time
| streamstats window=288 current=t first(pool_percent_used) AS pool_24h_ago BY cluster_name, pool_name
| eval g24=if((isnotnull(pool_24h_ago)) AND (isnotnull(pool_percent_used)), pool_percent_used - pool_24h_ago, null())
| eval hr_growth=if((isnotnull(g24)) AND (g24>0), g24/24, null())
| eval eta_to_full_h=if((isnotnull(hr_growth)) AND (hr_growth>0) AND (pool_percent_used<0.95), round((0.95 - pool_percent_used) / hr_growth, 2), null())
| eval eta_to_full_h=if((isnotnull(g24)) AND (g24<=0), null(), eta_to_full_h)
| stats max(_time) AS _time latest(pool_percent_used) AS pool_percent_used latest(eta_to_full_h) AS eta_to_full_h BY cluster_name, pool_name
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [ search (index=hci OR index=ceph) sourcetype="proxmox:ceph:osd" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, pve_cluster, ""))
      | eval osd_t=tonumber(toString(coalesce(osd_id, id, "0")), 10)
      | eval osd_tu=tonumber(toString(coalesce(utilization, utilization_percent, "0")), 10)
      | eval o_pct=if((isnotnull(osd_tu)) AND (osd_tu>1), osd_tu/100, coalesce(osd_tu, 0))
      | sort 0 cluster_name, -o_pct
      | dedup cluster_name
      | eval worst_osd_id=osd_t
      | eval worst_osd_pct=o_pct
      | table cluster_name, worst_osd_id, worst_osd_pct
    ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [ search (index=hci OR index=ceph) sourcetype="proxmox:ceph:status" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, pve_cluster, ""))
      | sort 0 - _time
      | dedup cluster_name
      | eval ceph_health=toString(upper(coalesce(health__status, health_status, "HEALTH_OK")))
      | eval nearfull_pool_count=tonumber(toString(coalesce(nearfull_pool_count, n_pool_nearfull, "0")), 10)
      | eval nearfull_osd_count=tonumber(toString(coalesce(nearfull_osd_count, n_osd_nearfull, "0")), 10)
      | eval mqc=tonumber(toString(coalesce(mon_quorum_lost, mon_ql, "0")), 10)
      | table cluster_name, ceph_health, nearfull_pool_count, nearfull_osd_count, mqc
    ]
| fillnull value=0 mqc
| fillnull value=0 nearfull_pool_count
| fillnull value=0 nearfull_osd_count
| fillnull value="HEALTH_OK" ceph_health
| eval worst_osd_pct=round(coalesce(worst_osd_pct, 0), 6)
| eval w=max(pool_percent_used, worst_osd_pct)
| eval critical_hit=if((ceph_health="HEALTH_ERR") OR (w>=0.95), 1, 0)
| eval high_hit=if((w>=0.90) OR (nearfull_osd_count>1) OR (mqc=1) OR ((ceph_health="HEALTH_WARN") AND ((nearfull_pool_count>0) OR (nearfull_osd_count>0))), 1, 0)
| eval medium_hit=if((w>=0.85) OR (nearfull_pool_count>=1) OR (nearfull_osd_count=1), 1, 0)
| eval low_hit=if((w>=0.75) AND (w<0.85), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case((critical_hit=1), "critical", (high_hit=1), "high", (medium_hit=1), "medium", (low_hit=1), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| lookup proxmox_ceph_owner cluster_name, pool_name OUTPUT expected_replication, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, expansion_planned_date
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(ft, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval no_freeze_bypass=if((ceph_health="HEALTH_ERR") OR (pool_percent_used>=0.95) OR (worst_osd_pct>=0.95), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (no_freeze_bypass=0), "ok", severity_bucket)
| sort 0 cluster_name, pool_name
| table cluster_name, pool_name, pool_percent_used, worst_osd_pct, worst_osd_id, nearfull_pool_count, nearfull_osd_count, ceph_health, eta_to_full_h, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email
description: Correlates Proxmox-managed Ceph RBD pool utilisation, per-OSD utilisation, and monitor-threshold state from custom HEC sourcetypes (proxmox:ceph:pool, proxmox:ceph:osd, proxmox:ceph:status) with coalesce for snake_case and camelCase, lookup enrichment from proxmox_ceph_owner, five-tier severity against Ceph's near-to-full sequence (0.75 planning band, 0.85 NEARFULL class, 0.90 backfill cap, 0.95 write block) plus HEALTH_ERR, NEARFULL counts, and mon quorum loss, freeze-aware demotion that never masks HEALTH_ERR or at-or-above full-ratio utilisation, and a 24h streamstats-based ETA to the default full class at 0.95 when growth is positive. The pattern differs from vSAN (UC-19.2.27), vSAN disk groups (UC-19.2.17), and Nutanix (UC-19.2.25) by centering PVE's REST, RBD pool math, and explicit OSD hot-spot risk.
value: A cluster-wide average can stay green while one OSD is already in Ceph's NEARFULL or FULL class, which blocks or harms the placement groups that land on that device—virtual disks stop accepting writes, guests error, and recovery gets harder the longer you wait. Seeing pool-level utilisation, worst OSD, monitor health, and a stable ownership row together gives the storage and virtualization teams a shared, early signal with time to add disks, reweight, or expand the rack before a forced, risky emergency change. It also makes finance freezes and planned expansions from the same lookup part of the routing story instead of a surprise override.
implementation: Deploy the Python 3 HEC poller to Heavy Forwarder with TLS verification, PVEAPIToken from vault, three sourcetype streams, props.conf for JSON at UTC, proxmox_ceph_owner.csv with transforms, run the saved search on a 5–15m schedule with earliest=-2h@h, wire alerts by severity, and add optional nix-based ceph CLI for gap-fill. Confirm monitor ratio defaults in ceph config match what you document to operators.
visualization: Single values for health-check class counts, sortable table of the alert output columns, timechart of pool growth for RBD production pools, scatter of OSD weight versus util for skew, and optional ITSI service tiles when premiumApps includes Splunk ITSI.

references.title: PVE: Deploy a hyper-converged Ceph cluster
Ceph: Monitoring OSDs and placement groups

knownFalsePositives: A capacity expansion or reweight in flight can make pool_percent_used wobble for hours while pgs are remapped and backfill runs—treat a mid-rebalance spike as a planning signal, not a failed disk. A guest deleting large RBD images can make g24 negative and null the ETA while Ceph is still rebalancing, which looks like 'no growth' in Splunk but is good news. CephFS-heavy clusters may flag metadata pools in ways that RBD-only operators do not care about; filter to business pools when noise persists. Throttled recovery or an offline MON during rolling upgrades can flash WARN without true capacity stress—use poller debouncing for quorum. Erasure pools change how much raw space a logical terabyte needs; mis-set expected_replication in the lookup can make owners think replication is wrong. BlueStore deferred discard can delay free-space reflection compared to a df on a single OSD. Lab clusters with only two OSDs will show uninteresting variance. These patterns are specific to Ceph and PVE, not a vSAN slack-reserve or Nutanix curator score case.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional: entity per cluster, KPIs for max OSD util and NEARFULL counts

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

Proxmox VE (PVE) is an open-source hyperconverged stack: the same nodes often run the KVM and LXC host service plus either ZFS (local) or a Ceph cluster for shared RBD (Rados block device) storage for virtual disks. Ceph is a distributed object, block, and file store. In a PVE deployment, a pool is a logical RBD (or CephFS) namespace with a replication or erasure policy, placement rules, and PG layout; an OSD (Object Storage Daemon) is the per-disk Ceph process that actually stores objects. A single OSD that crosses Ceph's capacity ratios (NEARFULL, BACKFILLFULL, FULL) can block writes to placement groups that map there even when the sum of the pool's bytes_used divided by available space (the headline pool percentage) still looks comfortable, because of distribution skew, CRUSH weight imbalance, or a new empty node with low weight.

Ceph's monitor-enforced sequence uses mon_osd_nearfull_ratio (default 0.85) for the first pressure class, mon_osd_backfillfull_ratio (default 0.90) so backfill stops, mon_osd_full_ratio (default 0.95) for pool- or OSD-FULL, and osd_failsafe_full_ratio (default 0.97) as a last line at the OSD. The time from first NEARFULL to FULL depends on data growth, rebalance, and how aggressively you add disks or reweight.

When Ceph returns FULL, protected writes fail, RBD and QEMU layers surface I/O errors, and guests can fail; recovery is hard without capacity, reweight, or, only as a last emergency, changing replication. Monitoring only host disk free percent misses CRUSH, replication, and EC math. The lookup proxmox_ceph_owner (CSV proxmox_ceph_owner.csv) columns: cluster_name, pool_name, expected_replication, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, expansion_planned_date. Use inputlookup proxmox_ceph_owner in Search to review rows; the alert uses lookup for enrichment, not a pure inputlookup-only pipeline.

Ceph is not a Splunk CIM use case. This scope is distinct from vSAN (UC-19.2.27), vSAN disk groups (UC-19.2.17), and Nutanix (UC-19.2.25).

Step 1 — Configure data collection

PVE has no first-party Splunk TA. Create PVEAPIToken: pveum user add splunk_reader@pve; pveum acl modify / -user splunk_reader@pve -role PVEAuditor; pveum user token add splunk_reader@pve splunk_token. Store the secret in a vault. Example: curl -k -H "Authorization: PVEAPIToken=splunk_reader@pve!splunk_token=SECRET" "https://<pve>:8006/api2/json/cluster/ceph/pools" — in production use --cacert. Handle HTTP 401 (rotate), 429 (back off), 596/597 (TLS) with retries. HEC: token on indexer; index hci or index ceph; TRUNCATE=0 in props. Schedule 300s (capacity) or 60s (OSD var). Python 3.10+ poller, urllib.request, ssl.create_default_context() then load_verify_locations on your CA bundle; never use ssl._create_unverified_context.

inputs.conf example:

[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_pve_ceph/bin/pve_ceph_poll.py]
python.version = python3
interval = 300
sourcetype = manual
index = hci
source = pve:ceph:poll
disabled = 0

Complete reference poller (stdlib only; extend logging and list_nodes in production):

```python
import json, os, sys, time, ssl, urllib.request, urllib.error, urllib.parse

PVE = os.environ.get("PVE_BASE", "https://pve01.example:8006")
TOK = os.environ.get("PVEAPIToken", "splunk_reader@pve!splunk_token=REDACTED")
HEC = os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HEC_URL", "https://idx:8088/services/collector/event")
HEC_TOK = os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HEC", "REDACTED")
CA = os.environ.get("PVE_CA_BUNDLE", "/etc/pki/tls/certs/pve-and-org-chain.pem")
CLUSTER = os.environ.get("PVE_CLUSTER_NAME", "pve-ceph-prod")

def pve_ctx():
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
    if CA and os.path.isfile(CA):
        ctx.load_verify_locations(CA)
    return ctx

def pve_get(path, ctx):
    url = PVE.rstrip("/") + path
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"Authorization": "PVEAPIToken " + TOK})
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=45) as r:
            return r.status, r.read()
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        if e.code in (401, 403):
            raise SystemExit("PVE API auth failed — rotate PVEAPIToken")
        if e.code == 429:
            time.sleep(5)
            return pve_get(path, ctx)
        raise
    except ssl.SSLError as e:
        msg = str(e)
        if "596" in msg or "597" in msg:
            time.sleep(2)
            return pve_get(path, ctx)
        raise

def hec_send(events, st):
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
    for ev in events:
        body = json.dumps(
            {"index": "hci", "sourcetype": st, "source": "pve:ceph:poll", "time": int(time.time()), "event": ev}
        ).encode("utf-8")
        h = {"Authorization": "Splunk " + HEC_TOK, "Content-Type": "application/json"}
        req = urllib.request.Request(HEC, data=body, headers=h, method="POST")
        urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=30).read()

def main():
    ctx = pve_ctx()
    st, raw = pve_get("/api2/json/cluster/ceph/pools", ctx)
    pools = json.loads(raw.decode("utf-8"))["data"]
    pe = []
    for p in pools or []:
        row = dict(p)
        row["cluster_name"] = CLUSTER
        pe.append(row)
    st2, sraw = pve_get("/api2/json/cluster/ceph/status", ctx)
    status = json.loads(sraw.decode("utf-8"))["data"]
    se = dict(status)
    se["cluster_name"] = CLUSTER
    hec_send(pe, "proxmox:ceph:pool")
    hec_send([se], "proxmox:ceph:status")
    nst, nraw = pve_get("/api2/json/nodes", ctx)
    nodes = json.loads(nraw.decode("utf-8"))["data"]
    oevents = []
    for n in nodes or []:
        nname = n["node"]
        path = "/api2/json/nodes/%s/ceph/osd" % (urllib.parse.quote(nname, safe=""),)
        try:
            ost, oraw = pve_get(path, ctx)
            oj = json.loads(oraw.decode("utf-8"))["data"]
        except Exception:
            continue
        for o in oj or []:
            o2 = dict(o)
            o2["cluster_name"] = CLUSTER
            o2["host"] = nname
            oevents.append(o2)
    hec_send(oevents, "proxmox:ceph:osd")
    return 0

if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

props.conf (indexer or first parsing hop):

[proxmox:ceph:pool]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
TZ = UTC

[proxmox:ceph:osd]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
TZ = UTC

[proxmox:ceph:status]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
TZ = UTC

transforms.conf: [proxmox_ceph_owner] filename = proxmox_ceph_owner.csv, min_matches = 0

One emphasized term for this block: PVEAPIToken is the only supported API auth in the documented poller path.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Schedule on earliest=-2h@h. The canonical SPL is the spl field. Row routing: verify lookup keys match. Use lookup in the pipeline; inputlookup is for ad-hoc CSV review of proxmox_ceph_owner. Pipeline walk: pool branch streamstats for 24h at 5m; joins bring worst OSD and cluster status; case sets five tiers; freeze demotes only when no_freeze_bypass is 0 (HEALTH_ERR and util at or past full ratio do not get demoted).

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.33 — Proxmox PVE Ceph: pool+OSD capacity vs mon_osd_nearfull_ratio mon_osd_backfillfull_ratio mon_osd_full_ratio; PVE API HEC (proxmox:ceph:pool, proxmox:ceph:osd, proxmox:ceph:status). NOT vSAN, NOT Nutanix, NOT S2D. Pollers emit snake_case; coalesce for camelCase. ETA uses 24h streamstats; freeze demotes only when not HEALTH_ERR AND below full ratios; AND OR boolean keywords in logic.")`
(index=hci OR index=ceph) sourcetype="proxmox:ceph:pool" earliest=-2h@h
| eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, pve_cluster, ""))
| eval pool_name=toString(coalesce(pool_name, poolName, name, ""))
| eval pool_t=tonumber(toString(coalesce(percent_used, percentUsed, "0")), 10)
| eval pool_percent_used=if((isnotnull(pool_t)) AND (pool_t>1), round(pool_t/100, 6), round(coalesce(pool_t, 0), 6))
| sort 0 + cluster_name, pool_name, _time
| streamstats window=288 current=t first(pool_percent_used) AS pool_24h_ago BY cluster_name, pool_name
| eval g24=if((isnotnull(pool_24h_ago)) AND (isnotnull(pool_percent_used)), pool_percent_used - pool_24h_ago, null())
| eval hr_growth=if((isnotnull(g24)) AND (g24>0), g24/24, null())
| eval eta_to_full_h=if((isnotnull(hr_growth)) AND (hr_growth>0) AND (pool_percent_used<0.95), round((0.95 - pool_percent_used) / hr_growth, 2), null())
| eval eta_to_full_h=if((isnotnull(g24)) AND (g24<=0), null(), eta_to_full_h)
| stats max(_time) AS _time latest(pool_percent_used) AS pool_percent_used latest(eta_to_full_h) AS eta_to_full_h BY cluster_name, pool_name
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [ search (index=hci OR index=ceph) sourcetype="proxmox:ceph:osd" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, pve_cluster, ""))
      | eval osd_t=tonumber(toString(coalesce(osd_id, id, "0")), 10)
      | eval osd_tu=tonumber(toString(coalesce(utilization, utilization_percent, "0")), 10)
      | eval o_pct=if((isnotnull(osd_tu)) AND (osd_tu>1), osd_tu/100, coalesce(osd_tu, 0))
      | sort 0 cluster_name, -o_pct
      | dedup cluster_name
      | eval worst_osd_id=osd_t
      | eval worst_osd_pct=o_pct
      | table cluster_name, worst_osd_id, worst_osd_pct
    ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [ search (index=hci OR index=ceph) sourcetype="proxmox:ceph:status" earliest=-2h@h
      | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, pve_cluster, ""))
      | sort 0 - _time
      | dedup cluster_name
      | eval ceph_health=toString(upper(coalesce(health__status, health_status, "HEALTH_OK")))
      | eval nearfull_pool_count=tonumber(toString(coalesce(nearfull_pool_count, n_pool_nearfull, "0")), 10)
      | eval nearfull_osd_count=tonumber(toString(coalesce(nearfull_osd_count, n_osd_nearfull, "0")), 10)
      | eval mqc=tonumber(toString(coalesce(mon_quorum_lost, mon_ql, "0")), 10)
      | table cluster_name, ceph_health, nearfull_pool_count, nearfull_osd_count, mqc
    ]
| fillnull value=0 mqc
| fillnull value=0 nearfull_pool_count
| fillnull value=0 nearfull_osd_count
| fillnull value="HEALTH_OK" ceph_health
| eval worst_osd_pct=round(coalesce(worst_osd_pct, 0), 6)
| eval w=max(pool_percent_used, worst_osd_pct)
| eval critical_hit=if((ceph_health="HEALTH_ERR") OR (w>=0.95), 1, 0)
| eval high_hit=if((w>=0.90) OR (nearfull_osd_count>1) OR (mqc=1) OR ((ceph_health="HEALTH_WARN") AND ((nearfull_pool_count>0) OR (nearfull_osd_count>0))), 1, 0)
| eval medium_hit=if((w>=0.85) OR (nearfull_pool_count>=1) OR (nearfull_osd_count=1), 1, 0)
| eval low_hit=if((w>=0.75) AND (w<0.85), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case((critical_hit=1), "critical", (high_hit=1), "high", (medium_hit=1), "medium", (low_hit=1), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| lookup proxmox_ceph_owner cluster_name, pool_name OUTPUT expected_replication, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, expansion_planned_date
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(ft, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval no_freeze_bypass=if((ceph_health="HEALTH_ERR") OR (pool_percent_used>=0.95) OR (worst_osd_pct>=0.95), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (no_freeze_bypass=0), "ok", severity_bucket)
| sort 0 cluster_name, pool_name
| table cluster_name, pool_name, pool_percent_used, worst_osd_pct, worst_osd_id, nearfull_pool_count, nearfull_osd_count, ceph_health, eta_to_full_h, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email
```

Step 3 — Validate

(1) Proxmox: Datacenter, Ceph, Pools — match pool % used to proxmox:ceph:pool. (2) Datacenter, Ceph, OSD view — per-OSD % to proxmox:ceph:osd. (3) On a PVE node shell: ceph df detail, ceph osd df, ceph status — match pool, OSD, and health. (4) API: curl -k -H "Authorization: PVEAPIToken=..." "https://<pve>:8006/api2/json/cluster/ceph/pools" and the status endpoint; compare to HEC. (5) If UI and SPL disagree: sampling lag between polls, CRUSH reweight during backfill, newly added OSDs not yet in average variance, or different math (replicated vs raw bytes). This path is not a vSphere or Prism validation.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboard: single values for pool rows in NEARFULL, BACKFILLFULL, FULL (from health.checks in proxmox:ceph:status); table of OSDs at risk; timechart of pool_percent_used; scatter of OSD weight vs utilisation. Alerts: PagerDuty for POOL_FULL, OSD_FULL, or ceph HEALTH_ERR; ServiceNow P2 for BACKFILLFULL or HEALTH_WARN with NEARFULL-style checks; Slack for first NEARFULL; email digest for low band. ITSI: one service per cluster, KPIs max_osd_pct, pool nearfull count, ceph_health as numeric. Runbooks: one OSD in NEARFULL (reweight, add OSD, or drain); many pools in NEARFULL (add capacity cluster-wide, avoid replication cuts until last resort); confirmed FULL and writes blocked (evac, expand, emergency procedures only with approval).

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

(a) Empty: verify HEC token, PVEAPIToken, test curl, check hf/poller logs. (b) UI warns but health is OK in index: health.checks field paths differ by Ceph release; add coalesce in the poller. (c) Missing OSD: older PVE 7.x API gap; use Splunk_TA_nix ceph osd df to proxmox:ceph:osd_df. (d) Pool % mismatch: UI can show post-replication or effective store; check bytes_used, max_avail, and crush rule size; EC alters the story. (e) eta_to_full_h negative: growth g24 is negative; treat ETA as null. (f) Freeze demotes FULL: fix no_freeze_bypass; POOL_FULL, OSD_FULL, HEALTH_ERR are never downgraded. (g) var=1.0: few OSDs or perfect balance. (h) EC: effective factor (k plus m) over k. (i) rbd vs cephfs vs metadata: filter in SPL if only VM RBD scope. (j) PVE 7.4 vs 8.x: confirm JSON with curl. (k) Post-upgrade auth: pveum user token list; re-issue token. (l) Quorum: debounce mon_quorum_lost in poller if MON election during rolling work.
grandmaExplanation: We keep watch on how full the shared storage is on your Proxmox Ceph setup before the virtual machines can no longer write safely. A single overfull drive can break things even when the overall number still looks fine, so we look at the highest-risk disk as well. We also respect planned freeze windows, except when a real outage-level health or full condition would be dangerous to ignore.
hardware: Targets Proxmox VE 7.4+ and 8.x on server hardware that matches your team's Ceph and PVE support posture: single-socket and dual-socket Supermicro Ultra, Dell PowerEdge R640 through R760 lines, HPE ProLiant DL360 and DL380 Gen10 through Gen11, Lenovo ThinkSystem SR635 and SR650, or Fujitsu PRIMERGY RX and dual-port-NVMe balanced builds. Ceph capacity OSDs are typically large SATA or SAS SSD, enterprise HDD, or hybrid with NVMe for BlueStore; cache or DB/WAL on NVMe when the Ceph day-one design calls for it. Network is usually 10GbE to 25/100 GbE for Ceph public and separate cluster backplanes on larger sites. PVE and Ceph versions (Pacific, Quincy, Reef) should stay within the matrix your vendor documents for the same cluster—mixed major Ceph on the same fsid is not a Splunk problem first, it is a cluster support problem, but the use case still applies so long as the API returns the fields you coalesce. Storage controllers must be on the Ceph and PVE HCL; consumer SSDs in production OSDs will generate misleading utilisation and latency noise unrelated to this SPL.
